text: The part that gets lost in stories like this is how normal it feels inside the company. Nobody plans to ship bad updates. It just becomes the default when the incentives point that way.

text: This happens more than people realize. Systems designed to detect fraud end up punishing the people who can least afford it. A 150 dollar hold can ruin someone's week.
original_text_preview: Hey @PayPal @AskPayPal I'm not laundering 150 USD. I'm a software engineer.

text: The trust gap here is real. People can tell when something was written by a machine even before they finish reading it. That instinct may matter more than the quality of the output.

text: The framing here is wrong. Knowing how to bypass something has nothing to do with being a good engineer. The best engineers I have worked with spent most of their time thinking, not hacking around things.

text: Stories like this remind me that the hiring process is still mostly luck and timing. Talent is everywhere. Opportunity is not. The systems we build to find people still miss most of them.
